title: inula × sericata
intro: Inula × sericata, also known as elecampane, is a hybrid perennial plant in the dandelion family that has been used in traditional medicine for its unique properties. It contains sesquiterpene lactones, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of elecampane have been used to alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of elecampane, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, are commonly used to promote digestive health and soothe respiratory issues.
